reasoning: The Lean statement captures all three conditions correctly: (1) `c : ℕ → EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin 2)` gives an infinite sequence of centers in ℝ² with no cluster point via `¬ ∃ p, MapClusterPt p atTop c`, which means no subsequence converges — the standard reading of "no limit point in the finite plane". (2) `Summable (fun i ↦ r i ^ 2)` is equivalent to finite sum of areas (areas = π r_i² differ only by the positive constant π); even if Lean allows r_i ∈ ℝ (not just ≥ 0), the squaring and the `closedBall` behavior (empty for r < 0) makes the existence statement equivalent to the informal one by replacing negative radii with 0. (3) `AffineSubspace ℝ (EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin 2))` with `Module.finrank ℝ L.direction = 1` exactly picks out lines: the empty subspace ⊥ has direction ⊥ with finrank 0, so the finrank-1 constraint forces a non-empty 1-dimensional affine subspace (a line). The encoding as `statement ↔ putnam_1991_a4_solution` (with solution to be `True`) is the standard Putnam "does there exist" framing.
